# Local IPC Endpoint Model

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/distributionhostlocalipcdocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to distribution host local IPC](../local-ipc.md)

`internal/hostintegration.LocalIPCEndpoint` and `DefaultLocalIPCEndpoint` own the C11 endpoint descriptor. They calculate local-only endpoint shape and path; they do not open sockets, named pipes, or HTTP listeners.

```text
LocalIPCEndpoint {
    channel      DistributionChannel
    hostOS       "darwin" | "windows"
    endpointKind "unix-socket" | "named-pipe"
    path         string
    owner        "store-app" | "helper"
}
```

| Channel | IPC adapter | Path owner |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `developer-id` | Unix domain socket | `~/Library/Application Support/riido` or user config dir |
| `mac-app-store` | Unix domain socket inside app group/container | App Sandbox container or app group |
| `msix-sideload` | Windows named pipe | Package local data / app identity |
| `msix-store` | Windows named pipe | Package local data / app identity |
| `dev-local` | Unix domain socket | Current launchd/dev path |

- `cmd/riido` default daemon socket is computed through C11 `AppDataRoot` plus `LocalIPCEndpoint`.
- Windows named-pipe transport reuses the same request envelope and `riidoapi` handler shape.
- C1 through C10 must not import OS-specific listener adapters.
